5321	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO,
5322	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis} (proof).}
5323	  Let \(s\) be a cover system on a basis for \(X\) (Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system}). For every
5324	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) that has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)
5325	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}), every basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\), and
5326	  every \(p > 0\), the absolute cohomology vanishes,
5327	  \[
5328	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0,
5329	  \]
5330	  where \(H^p(U, -)\) is the Ext realization of Definition~\ref{def:absolute_cohomology}. The
5331	  formal statement carries \([\,\mathrm{EnoughInjectives}\ X.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\) as an explicit
5332	  instance hypothesis: enough injectives in the category of sheaves of
5333	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is needed to embed \(\mathcal{F}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{I}\), and
5334	  that instance is not currently available in the ambient library for sheaves of modules (it would
5335	  follow from \(\mathrm{IsGrothendieckAbelian}(\mathrm{SheafOfModules}\ R)\)).
5336	\end{lemma}

5338	\begin{proof}
5339	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech,
5340	    lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses, lem:cech_ses_of_basis,
5341	    lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib, lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
5342	  Induct on \(p \ge 1\) with the statement quantified over \emph{all} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
5343	  \(\mathcal{F}\) having vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)
5344	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) simultaneously, so that the quotient may take the
5345	  role of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the next stage. Fix such an \(\mathcal{F}\), embed
5346	  \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and put
5347	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving a short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F}
5348	  \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\).
5349	
5350	  We first record the geometric input that both the quotient-closure and the base case need. For
5351	  every \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\), the faces-in-basis field \(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\) (condition
5352	  (1)) places every face \(V_\sigma\) in \(s.\mathcal{B}\); the section-surjectivity field
5353	  \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) of the cover system, applied to the short exact sequence \(S\) at
5354	  \(V_\sigma\), then gives section surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\).
5355	  Its left-term {\v C}ech-vanishing hypothesis \(\check{H}^{>0}(-,\mathcal{F}) = 0\) over the coverings
5356	  of \(V_\sigma\) is supplied by \(\mathcal{F}\)'s vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology
5357	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}); mathematically this field packages
5358	  Lemma~\ref{lem:ses_cech_h1} (cofinality plus the {\v C}ech \(\check{H}^1\)-surjectivity criterion).
5359	  By the per-face derivation
5360	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}) the per-face short complexes of \(S\) are short exact, so
5361	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} gives the short exact sequence of section {\v C}ech complexes for
5362	  \(\mathcal{U}\); in particular the section surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U)\) holds
5363	  at every basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\) (take \(\mathcal{U}\) a covering of \(U\) in
5364	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov}\) and the length-one face). Feeding the {\v C}ech short exact sequence into
5365	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech} — with \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\)
5366	  from injective acyclicity and \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) from
5367	  \(\mathcal{F}\)'s hypothesis — shows \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for every
5368	  \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\); that is, \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has vanishing higher {\v C}ech
5369	  cohomology for \(s\), so it is itself a valid instance of the inductive statement.
5370	
5371	  \emph{Base case \(p = 1\):} apply Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing} to \(S\) at
5372	  the basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\), its section-surjectivity hypothesis discharged by the
5373	  paragraph above.
5374	
5375	  \emph{Step \(p \Rightarrow p+1\):} the covariant Ext long exact sequence
5376	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) of
5377	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) at \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\)
5378	  gives the exact strand
5379	  \[
5380	    H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) \xrightarrow{\ \delta\ } H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) \to
5381	      H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{I}).
5382	  \]
5383	  The right term vanishes since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective
5384	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}), and the left term
5385	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) by the inductive hypothesis applied to \(\mathcal{Q}\)
5386	  (admissible by the previous paragraph). Exactness forces
5387	  \(H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This completes the induction, giving
5388	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and all \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\).
5389	\end{proof}

5421	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
5422	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.}
5423	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
5424	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
5425	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :
5426	  U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) has \(U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}\) and
5427	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
5428	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
5429	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
5430	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
5431	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then
5432	  \[
5433	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0 \text{ and any }
5434	      U \in \mathcal{B}.
5435	  \]
5436	  This is the basis-comparison vehicle that upgrades the standard-cover {\v C}ech
5437	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of
5438	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}: instantiating \(\mathcal{B}\) as the affine
5439	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly
5440	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. The formal target of this lemma is the
5441	  \emph{vanishing} conclusion \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\) under
5442	  hypotheses (1)--(3), not an explicit isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
5443	  \mathcal{F}) \cong H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of {\v C}ech and sheaf cohomology; only
5444	  the positive-degree vanishing is needed downstream, and it is all the statement
5445	  establishes. The formal statement carries
5446	  \([\,\mathrm{EnoughInjectives}\ X.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\) as an explicit instance hypothesis,
5447	  inherited from the dimension-shift induction (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}):
5448	  it is needed to embed the coefficient module into an injective, and that instance is not currently
5449	  available in the ambient library for sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (it would follow from
5450	  \(\mathrm{IsGrothendieckAbelian}(\mathrm{SheafOfModules}\ R)\)).
5451	\end{lemma}

5456	% Choose an embedding $\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I}$ into an
5457	% injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.

5464	% $$

5510	\begin{proof}
5511	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech,
5512	    lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing, def:absolute_cohomology}
5513	    \leanok
5514	  Here \(H^p(U, -)\) denotes the Ext realization of absolute sheaf cohomology fixed in
5515	  Definition~\ref{def:absolute_cohomology}. The proof is the thin assembly of the sub-lemmas through
5516	  the cover-system encoding. Conditions (1)--(2) of the hypotheses are exactly the faces-in-basis and
5517	  cofinality data of a cover system on a basis, so \(\mathcal{B}\) and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) assemble into
5518	  an instance \(s\) of Definition~\ref{def:basis_cov_system} with \(s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and
5519	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov} = \mathrm{Cov}\); condition (3) is exactly the assertion that \(\mathcal{F}\) has
5520	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\) (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}).
5521	
5522	  The conclusion is then the dimension-shift induction
5523	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing} applied to this instance: for every basic open
5524	  \(U \in s.\mathcal{B} = \mathcal{B}\) and every \(p > 0\) one has \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). That
5525	  induction internally embeds \(\mathcal{F}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{I}\), forms the quotient
5526	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), keeps \(\mathcal{Q}\) inside the inductive class via the
5527	  {\v C}ech short exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis}) and its quotient-stability
5528	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech}), and bottoms out at the base case
5529	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}).
5530	
5531	  The argument never uses affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing
5532	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is condition (3),
5533	  which on the affine instantiation is supplied by the standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
5534	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. Thus the dependency
5535	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the graph is
5536	  acyclic.
5537	\end{proof}
5538	
5539	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
5540	
5541	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
5542	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
5543	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
5544	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
5545	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
5546	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
5547	
5548	\begin{lemma}
5549	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
5550	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
5551	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
5552	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
5553	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
5554	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
5555	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
5556	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
5557	  % \begin{equation}
5558	  % \label{equation-extended}
5559	  % 0 \to
5560	  % M \to
5561	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
5562	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
5563	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
5564	  % \ldots
5565	  % \end{equation}
5566	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
5567	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
5568	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
5569	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
5570	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
5571	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
5572	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
5573	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
5574	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
5575	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
5576	  \[
5577	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
5578	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
5579	    \qquad
5580	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
5581	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
5582	  \]
5583	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
5584	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
5585	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
5586	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
5587	\end{lemma}
5588	
5589	\begin{proof}
5590	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
5591	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
5592	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
5593	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
5594	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
5595	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
5596	  localisations
5597	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
5598	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
5599	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
5600	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
5601	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
5602	\end{proof}
5603	
5604	\begin{lemma}
5605	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
5606	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
5607	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
5608	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
5609	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
5610	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
5611	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
5612	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
5613	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
5614	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
5615	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
5616	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
5617	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
5618	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
5619	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
5620	  \[
5621	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
5622	      \;\cong\;
5623	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
5624	  \]
5625	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
5626	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
5627	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
5628	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
5629	\end{lemma}
5630	
5631	\begin{proof}
5632	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
5633	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
5634	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
5635	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
5636	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
5637	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
5638	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
5639	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
5640	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
5641	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
5642	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
5643	  additive homology-preserving functor.
5644	\end{proof}

5646	\begin{lemma}
5647	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
5648	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
5649	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
5650	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
5651	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
5652	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
5653	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
5654	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
5655	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
5656	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
5657	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
5658	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
5659	  % associated to the presheaves
5660	  % $$
5661	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
5662	  % $$
5663	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
5664	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
5665	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
5666	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
5667	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
5668	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
5669	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
5670	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
5671	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
5672	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
5673	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
5674	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
5675	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
5676	  \[
5677	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
5678	      \;\cong\;
5679	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
5680	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
5681	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
5682	  \]
5683	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
5684	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
5685	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
5686	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
5687	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
5688	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
5689	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
5690	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
5691	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
5692	
5693	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
5694	  of the preimage,
5695	  \[
5696	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
5697	      \;=\;
5698	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
5699	  \]
5700	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
5701	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
5702	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
5703	\end{lemma}
5704	
5705	\begin{proof}
5706	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
5707	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
5708	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
5709	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
5710	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
5711	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
5712	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
5713	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
5714	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
5715	  \[
5716	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
5717	      \;\cong\;
5718	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
5719	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
5720	  \]
5721	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
5722	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
5723	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
5724	\end{proof}
5725	
5726	\begin{lemma}
5727	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
5728	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
5729	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
5730	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
5731	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
5732	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
5733	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
5734	  % L180--199).
5735	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
5736	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
5737	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
5738	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
5739	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
5740	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
5741	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
5742	  \to S\). Then:
5743	  \begin{enumerate}
5744	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
5745	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
5746	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
5747	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
5748	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
5749	      \(k \geq 0\),
5750	      \[
5751	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
5752	      \]
5753	  \end{enumerate}
5754	\end{lemma}
5755	
5756	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
5757	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
5758	% the sheaf
5759	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
5760	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
5761	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
5762	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
5763	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
5764	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
5765	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
5766	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
5767	\begin{proof}
5768	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
5769	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
5770	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
5771	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
5772	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
5773	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
5774	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
5775	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
5776	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
5777	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
5778	
5779	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
5780	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
5781	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
5782	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
5783	
5784	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
5785	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
5786	  sheafification of
5787	  \[
5788	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
5789	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
5790	  \]
5791	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
5792	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
5793	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
5794	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
5795	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
5796	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
5797	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
5798	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
5799	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
5800	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
5801	
5802	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
5803	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
5804	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
5805	  for every \(k\),
5806	  \[
5807	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
5808	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
5809	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
5810	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
5811	  \]
5812	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
5813	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
5814	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
5815	\end{proof}

5817	\begin{lemma}
5818	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
5819	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
5820	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
5821	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
5822	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
5823	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
5824	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
5825	  % L180--199).
5826	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
5827	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.

5834	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =

5836	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
5837	  \[
5838	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
5839	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.

5845	% the sheaf
5846	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf

5851	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
5852	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
5853	% opens we win."
5854	\begin{proof}
5855	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
5856	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
5857	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
5858	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
5859	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
5860	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
5861	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
5862	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
5863	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
5864	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
5865	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
5866	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
5867	
5868	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
5869	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
5870	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
5871	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
5872	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
5873	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
5874	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
5875	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
5876	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
5877	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
5878	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
5879	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
5880	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
5881	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
5882	  \(k \geq 1\).
5883	\end{proof}

5917	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
5918	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
5919	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
5920	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
5921	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
5922	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
5923	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
5924	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
5925	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
5926	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
5927	  \[
5928	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
5929	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
5930	  \]
5931	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
5932	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
5933	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
5934	
5935	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
5936	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
5937	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
5938	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
5939	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in
5940	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\), and it compares the (always-defined) {\v C}ech higher
5941	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)
5942	  against the derived-functor one only in that case. Second, we assert the comparison in the
5943	  weak form of the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism, i.e.\ as
5944	  \(\bigl(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})) \cong
5945	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\) without singling out a chosen natural isomorphism.
5946	  This existence statement is all the downstream consumers (representability of the
5947	  relative Picard functor, local triviality of the comparison) require: they use
5948	  only that the unconditional {\v C}ech \(R^i f_*\) and the derived-functor
5949	  \(R^i f_*\) agree as objects, never a specific identification, so committing to a
5950	  canonical natural transformation would add coherence obligations that play no
5951	  role in the applications.
5952	\end{lemma}

5954	\begin{proof}
5955	  \uses{lem:cech_augmented_resolution, lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, def:cech_complex, def:higher_direct_image}
5956	  Apply the abstract Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} with the
5957	  right-derivable additive functor \(G = f_*\)
5958	  (the pushforward \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X) \to \operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)), the object
5959	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by
5960	  the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}. The two hypotheses of the
5961	  abstract lemma are exactly the geometric sub-lemmas established above:
5962	  \begin{enumerate}
5963	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution} the augmented {\v C}ech
5964	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is
5965	      exact, i.e.\ \(\mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
5966	      \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
5967	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic} each term
5968	      \(\mathcal{C}^p\) is right \(f_*\)-acyclic:
5969	      \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
5970	  \end{enumerate}
5971	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then gives, for every \(i\), an
5972	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th derived functor evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\) and
5973	  the \(i\)-th cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\) to the
5974	  resolution,
5975	  \[
5976	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})
5977	      \;\cong\;
5978	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).
5979	  \]
5980	  By construction (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}) the complex \(f_*\,
5981	  \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is precisely the relative {\v C}ech complex
5982	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so its \(i\)-th
5983	  cohomology is \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})
5984	  \bigr)\); and \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})\) is the
5985	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) of
5986	  Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}. This yields the isomorphism
5987	  \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr) \cong
5988	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) asserted by the lemma; taking it in the existence
5989	  (\(\operatorname{Nonempty}\)) form discards the choice of resolution, as required.
5990	  Over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), passing to global sections
5991	  recovers \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S,
5992	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\).

Now I'm checking the keystone lemma about quasi-coherent sections being localized modules on Spec R.  The restriction map from global sections to sections over a basic open D(f) is indeed an `IsLocalizedModule` at the powers of f. The dependencies chain through span-cover patching, finite basic-open refinement, quasi-coherence of free modules, and Čech acyclicity on affine covers—all of which check out as built and axiom-clean.

The proof strategy refines to a finite standard cover, uses the fact that free O-modules restrict to localized modules on each piece, applies Čech acyclicity to ensure agreement with localizations, then descends via the span-cover lemma. There's a subtle point about right-exactness of the section functor on the standard cover that needs careful handling. The Čech acyclicity result establishes that cohomology vanishes for tilde-modules on standard covers, but I need to be precise about how this connects to right-exactness of the section functor in the formalization. The crucial move is showing that for free modules, the restriction map is literally the localization map, then using this to lift the presentation through the Čech complex argument. Now I'm assembling the main lemma that shows the counit is an isomorphism, which relies on the localized module characterization as its foundation. The proof chains together the fact that the counit's restriction to D(f) recovers the localization map, uses that this is indeed an isomorphism between localized modules, and then reflects this back to the full category using the forgetful functor.

There's a technical subtlety emerging though: the forgetful functor being used here needs to be carefully specified—it's the one from sheaves of O_X-modules to sheaves of abelian groups, not to set-valued sheaves, and the argument that checking isomorphisms on a basis of distinguished opens suffices needs to be justified properly within this framework. isomorphisms in sheaves of modules (if the underlying abelian-group sheaf map is an iso, then the module morphism is too), and separately, that a sheaf morphism which is an iso on a basis of the topology is globally an iso.
